page_content:
for general classes of unstructured (single species discrete-time

and continuous-time, interacting populations etc.) and structured (spatially-structured, age- structured, sex-structured) population biology models in ecology and epidemiology, dynamics analysis of population biology models (asymptotic stability and bifurcation theory), numerical discretization of continuous-time models, statistical analysis (parameter estimation, uncertainty quantification).

Prerequisite: Calculus, differential equations, modeling, linear algebra, familiarity with mathematical software and programming languages (e.g., MATLAB, R, Python etc.); or permission of instructor.

Cross-listed with: AMSC721.
